message:
fix: add viewport scroll lock to prevent rocket scroll in alternate screen (#197)

xterm.js v6 processes writes asynchronously — between enqueue and write
callback, the browser may paint frames where xterm auto-scrolled the
viewport to cursor position. This manifests as 'rocket scroll' in tile
mode during heavy output from AI CLI tools.

Three-layer fix:
1. Persistent scroll event listener on .xterm-viewport enforces
   scrollTop=0 during alternate screen, catching ALL scroll sources
   (writes, fitAddon.fit(), resize) before the browser paints.
2. Write callback now re-checks buffer.active.type at callback time
   instead of using a captured value from enqueue time, handling
   screen transitions during async write processing.
3. scaleTiles setTimeout re-checks alternate screen inside the
   callback to prevent race with screen transitions during the 50ms
   delay.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
